channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: More travellers come forward as tour company cancellations mount
item.description: The ABC has been contacted by more than 100 travellers since first reporting on the matter on Monday, with some claiming their tours were cancelled at the last minute and itineraries changed.
item.dc:creator: Kristy Sexton-McGrath

media:group.media:description: Anthony Sheely was in Hong Kong when he was told his land tour with AVG Travels was cancelled.

item.title: Three nickel plants in WA to be converted to process gold
item.description: Idle infrastructure from WA's once-booming nickel industry is set to be retrofitted to produce gold bars amid historic high prices for the precious metal.
item.dc:creator: Jarrod Lucas and Tara de Landgrafft

media:group.media:description: Grant Haywood says: "Every man and his dog is putting through every stone that's got gold in it, so all the mills are full …"

item.title: Maldives investigates if Italian divers went too deep in fatal cave dive
item.description: A Maldives government official says a permit was given to study soft corals, but authorities were unaware the group planned to cave dive.

media:group.media:description: Monica Montefalcone and her daughter Giorgia Sommacal were among the divers who went missing in the Maldives.

item.title: Homesharer allegedly used disabled man's bank account to spend $54,000
item.description: A 70-year-old appointed homesharer is charged with 264 fraud offences after allegedly spending money belonging to an intellectually disabled man he was engaged to support.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Goerling

media:group.media:description: The man is due to face the Perth Magistrates Court on Wednesday charged with 264 fraud offences.

item.title: 'Backbone' of Victoria's bushfire, flood response to be cut, union claims
item.description: The State Control Centre, which responds to natural disasters like bushfires and floods, is to shed around two thirds of its full-time equivalent staff according to the Community and Public Sector Union.
item.dc:creator: Alex Sinnott

media:group.media:description: About 54 homes were destroyed in the bushfire in Harcourt earlier this year.

item.title: Teenager Bianca Adler becomes youngest Australian to summit Everest
item.description: A social media post just before 8am AEST announced that Bianca Adler had reached the top of the world's tallest mountain.

media:group.media:description: Bianca Adler this morning became the youngest Australian to summit Mount Everest.

item.title: Israeli forces fire at Gaza flotilla, all boats intercepted, organisers say
item.description: The Australian government says it is engaging with Israel over the welfare of 11 Australians detained, as Israeli forces fire "non-lethal" shots at two boats in a Gaza-bound aid flotilla.

media:group.media:description: An Israeli vessel approaches the flotilla.

item.title: Luai and Tigers headline NRL's Las Vegas quartet for 2027 season-openers
item.description: Jarome Luai will begin his final season with the Wests Tigers in Las Vegas, with the NRL unveiling its teams for next year's season-openers.

media:group.media:description: Jarome Luai will kick off his final season with the Wests Tigers with a trip to Las Vegas.

item.title: 'Numbers don't add up': Union slams ACT plan to boost paramedics, firefighters
item.description: The ACT government has unveiled a $94.3 million emergency services package, but the union says it still falls short by about 50 staff needed to prevent future station closures.
item.dc:creator: Lois Maskiell

media:group.media:description: Canberra is set to receive 32 new ambulance service roles and 35 new fire and rescue roles as a result of the funding.

item.title: Hunter advances in French Open qualifiers as seeded duo fall
item.description: Storm Hunter is a step closer to her first French Open main draw appearance since 2023, as Priscilla Hon and Maddison Inglis fall short in the qualifiers.

media:group.media:description: Storm Hunter is a step closer to booking her spot in the French Open main draw for the first time since 2023.

item.title: Fears mount for victims of domestic violence ahead of WA court closures
item.description: The doors of 26 regional and remote courthouses in WA will shut for three months, leaving lawyers confused and concerned about consequences for domestic violence legal matters.

media:group.media:description: The court house in Margaret River is one of 26 impacted by the closures.

item.title: Man charged with Arnhem Land murder bailed to live with family 500km away
item.description: A 20-year-old man charged with the murder of another man during a brawl in Arnhem Land last month has been bailed to live with family far from the location of the death, in the Tiwi Islands.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Barrett Peters

media:group.media:description: Milingimbi is an island community in Arnhem Land 440 kilometres east of Darwin in the Northern Territory.

item.title: Researchers uncover longest whale journey ever documented
item.description: Scientists analysing a database of photos find two humpback whales travelled from Queensland to Brazil, covering a record distance of 14,200km.
item.dc:creator: Nikki Sorbello

media:group.media:description: Researchers have found two humpback whales that travelled between Queensland and Brazil.

item.title: Fisheries body pushes for carp virus trial in Victoria's Wimmera
item.description: The Victorian Fisheries Authority wants to host a carp virus trial, but the federal government says it needs regulatory approval, which will delay research.
item.dc:creator: Timu King, Warwick Long, and Justine Longmore

media:group.media:description: Research for a carp herpes virus is set to run into 2028, a decade after the virus was initially slated for release.

item.title: Live: ASX falls as inflation worries lead to US government debt sell-off
item.description: The Australian share market fell after a US bond sell-off triggered further losses on Wall Street. Oil prices slipped after Donald Trump said he had been an hour away from ordering an attack on Iran before postponing it. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: David Chau

media:group.media:description: Investors are bracing for the end of the US Federal Reserve's coronavirus stimulus program.

item.title: Landscape in outback SA 'permanently changed' by recent earthquake
item.description: Cracks in the ground in outback Australia caused by a magnitude-5.5 earthquake could help researchers predict future seismic activity.
item.dc:creator: Tom Mann and Isabella Kelly

media:group.media:description: A rupture caused by the earthquake in the APY Lands last month.

item.title: More ISIS-linked women and children set to return to Australia
item.description: Six Australian women, along with their children and grandchildren, look likely to leave a camp in Syria within days.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Doran and Cherine Yazbeck

media:group.media:description: The Al Roj refugee camp houses people who were displaced by the fall of Islamic State in 2019.

item.title: 'Pure theft': Trump allies can seek payouts from $2.5b 'lawfare' fund
item.description: The US has set up a fund to compensate Americans deemed victims of "lawfare". Democrats say it is "pure theft of public funds" to pay the president's allies.
item.dc:creator: Brad Ryan

media:group.media:description: Donald Trump spent days in court when he was prosecuted for various alleged crimes before returning to the White House.

item.title: Live: Man dead, four injured in south-west Sydney shooting
item.description: A man has died and four others have been seriously injured following a Canley Heights shooting, which is believed to be organised crime-related.

media:group.media:description: NSW Police investigators on the scene of the shooting in Canley Heights.

item.title: Southampton expelled from Premier League playoffs for spying on rivals
item.description: Southampton loses its chance to gain promotion to the Premier League after being caught spying on Middlesbrough and two other rivals.

media:group.media:description: Southampton will not have the chance to gain promotion to the Premier League after unauthorised filming of Middlesbrough training sessions.

item.title: Six decades on and this Victorian community is still seeking its new bridge
item.description: Businesses relying on heavy transport to move goods across the Victoria-NSW border near Swan Hill say they are growing frustrated at the continual delays in replacing a 130-year-old wooden bridge.
item.dc:creator: Nethma Dandeniya

media:group.media:description: The Swan Hill Bridge was built in 1896 to accommodate a growing community.

item.title: San Diego mosque shooters met online, shared 'hatred'
item.description: Authorities have revealed the teenagers who killed three people at a San Diego mosque met online and shared a "broad hatred" toward different religions and races.

media:group.media:description: Three people were killed at the Islamic Center of San Diego.

item.title: How a 'partial obscuration' phenomenon is impacting Darwin's sunsets
item.description: A "beautiful" optical illusion has been impacting sunsets in Australia's north, with a little-known weather phenomenon appearing to split golden hour into different coloured beams.
item.dc:creator: Gemma Ferguson

media:group.media:description: This sunset was photographed from a balcony in Muirhead, in Darwin.

item.title: Live: Liberal frontbencher won't say if shadow cabinet was consulted on non-citizen welfare cuts
item.description: Shadow Health Minister Anne Ruston has not said whether shadow cabinet was consulted on a Coalition proposal to cut welfare to non-citizens, including permanent residents. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Boscaini

item.title: A mouse plague has broken out. Here's what you need to know
item.description: Mice in their millions are eating grain crops, destroying food, and invading houses. Here's how it all started.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Prendergast

media:group.media:description: Farmers and others in the community are battling to clean up dead mice daily.
